programme.title: Nobody
programme.desc: Hutch Mansell, a suburban dad, overlooked husband, nothing neighbor — a "nobody." When two thieves break into his home one night, Hutch's unknown long-simmering rage is ignited and propels him on a brutal path that will uncover dark secrets he fought to leave behind.

programme.title: Léon: The Professional
programme.desc: After her father, step-mother, step-sister and little brother are killed by her father's employers, the 12-year-old daughter of an abject drug dealer manages to take refuge in the apartment of a professional hitman who at her request teaches her the methods of his job so she can take her revenge on the corrupt DEA agent who ruined her life by killing her beloved brother.

programme.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga
programme.desc: After being snatched from the Green Place of Many Mothers, while the tyrants Dementus and Immortan Joe fight for power and control, the young Furiosa must survive many trials as she puts together the means to find her way home.

programme.title: Aliens
programme.desc: Ripley, the sole survivor of the Nostromo's deadly encounter with the monstrous Alien, returns to Earth after drifting through space in hypersleep for 57 years. Although her story is initially met with skepticism, she agrees to accompany a team of Colonial Marines back to LV-426.

programme.title: Ballerina
programme.desc: An assassin trained in the traditions of the Ruska Roma organization sets out to seek revenge after her father's death.

programme.title: Unforgiven
programme.desc: William Munny is a retired, once-ruthless killer turned gentle widower and hog farmer. To help support his two motherless children, he accepts one last bounty-hunter mission to find the men who brutalized a prostitute. Joined by his former partner and a cocky greenhorn, he takes on a corrupt sheriff.

programme.title: The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen
programme.desc: Renowned adventurer Allan Quatermain leads a team of extraordinary figures with legendary powers to battle the technological terror of a madman known as "The Fantom". This "League" comprises seafarer and inventor Captain Nemo, vampire Mina Harker, an invisible man named Rodney Skinner, American Secret Service Agent Tom Sawyer, the ageless and invincible Dorian Gray, and the dangerous split personality of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
programme.desc: After arriving in India, Indiana Jones is asked by a desperate village to find a mystical stone. He agrees – and stumbles upon a secret cult plotting a terrible plan in the catacombs of an ancient palace.

programme.title: The Chronicles of Riddick
programme.desc: The wanted criminal Richard Bruno Riddick arrives on a planet called Helion Prime and finds himself up against an invading empire called the Necromongers, an army that plans to convert or kill all humans in the universe.

programme.title: Nobody 2
programme.desc: Workaholic assassin Hutch Mansell takes his family on a much-needed vacation to the small tourist town of Plummerville. However, he soon finds himself in the crosshairs of a corrupt theme-park operator, a shady sheriff, and a bloodthirsty crime boss.

programme.title: Unforgiven
programme.desc: William Munny is a retired, once-ruthless killer turned gentle widower and hog farmer. To help support his two motherless children, he accepts one last bounty-hunter mission to find the men who brutalized a prostitute. Joined by his former partner and a cocky greenhorn, he takes on a corrupt sheriff.

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows
programme.desc: There is a new criminal mastermind at large (Professor Moriarty) and not only is he Holmes’ intellectual equal, but his capacity for evil and lack of conscience may give him an advantage over the detective.

programme.title: Underworld
programme.desc: Vampires and werewolves have waged a nocturnal war against each other for centuries. But all bets are off when a female vampire warrior named Selene, who's famous for her strength and werewolf-hunting prowess, becomes smitten with a peace-loving male werewolf, Michael, who wants to end the war.

programme.title: World Championship Wrestling - 3x22 - World Championship Wrestling 107
World Championship Wrestling - 3x23 - World Championship Wrestling 108
World Championship Wrestling - 3x24 - World Championship Wrestling 109
World Championship Wrestling - 3x25 - World Championship Wrestling 110
World Championship Wrestling - 3x26 - World Championship Wrestling 111
World Championship Wrestling - 3x27 - World Championship Wrestling 112
World Championship Wrestling - 3x28 - World Championship Wrestling 113
World Championship Wrestling - 3x29 - World Championship Wrestling 114
World Championship Wrestling - 3x30 - World Championship Wrestling 115
World Championship Wrestling - 3x31 - World Championship Wrestling 116
World Championship Wrestling - 3x32 - World Championship Wrestling 117
World Championship Wrestling - 3x33 - World Championship Wrestling 118
World Championship Wrestling - 3x34 - World Championship Wrestling 119
World Championship Wrestling - 3x35 - World Championship Wrestling 120
World Championship Wrestling - 3x36 - World Championship Wrestling 121
World Championship Wrestling - 3x37 - World Championship Wrestling 122
World Championship Wrestling - 3x38 - World Championship Wrestling 123
World Championship Wrestling - 3x39 - World Championship Wrestling 124
World Championship Wrestling - 3x40 - World Championship Wrestling 125
World Championship Wrestling - 3x41 - World Championship Wrestling 126
Revenge of the Nerds II: Nerds in Paradise
programme.desc: Get out your white socks! This time our suave college heroes hit the road for fun and fraternity in Florida. Another opportunity for brains to triumph over brawn. How can the girls possibly resist their geeky charms?

programme.title: Pee-wee's Big Adventure
programme.desc: The cartoonish and childish character Pee-wee Herman goes on a big adventure for the first time ever when his beloved shiny new bicycle is stolen by his nemesis Francis Buxton, a fellow man-child and neighborhood rich "kid." And he sets off on an obsessive cross-country journey, determined to recover it. Pee-wee's awkward and childish attempts to be cool and mature.

programme.title: Beetlejuice Beetlejuice
programme.desc: After a family tragedy, three generations of the Deetz family return home to Winter River. Still haunted by Beetlejuice, Lydia's life is turned upside down when her teenage daughter, Astrid, accidentally opens the portal to the Afterlife.

programme.title: Back to School
programme.desc: Millionaire businessman Thornton Melon is upset when his son Jason announces that he is not sure about going to college. Thornton insists that college is the best thing he never had for himself, and to prove his point, he agrees to enroll in school along with his son. Thornton is a big hit on campus: always throwing the biggest parties, knowing all the right people, but is this the way to pass college?

programme.title: Austin Powers in Goldmember
programme.desc: The world's most shagadelic spy continues his fight against Dr. Evil. This time, the diabolical doctor and his clone, Mini-Me, team up with a new foe—'70s kingpin Goldmember. While pursuing the team of villains to stop them from world domination, Austin gets help from his dad and an old girlfriend.

programme.title: Happy Gilmore
programme.desc: A Hockey player wannabe finds out that he has the most powerful golf drive in history. He joins the P.G.A. tour to make some money to save grandma's house. The downside is that his hockey player mentality doesn't really go on the P.G.A. tour. Especially with the favorite to win the championship.i

programme.title: The Toy
programme.desc: On one of his bratty son Eric's annual visits, the plutocrat U.S. Bates takes him to his department store and offers him anything in it as a gift. Eric chooses a black janitor who has made him laugh with his antics. At first the man suffers many indignities as Eric's "toy," but gradually teaches the lonely boy what it is like to have and to be a friend.

programme.title: Austin Powers: The Spy Who Shagged Me
programme.desc: When diabolical genius Dr. Evil travels back in time to steal superspy Austin Powers's ‘mojo,’ Austin must return to the swingin' '60s himself - with the help of American agent, Felicity Shagwell - to stop the dastardly plan. Once there, Austin faces off against Dr. Evil's army of minions to try to save the world in his own unbelievably groovy way.

programme.title: Monty Python and the Holy Grail 1974
programme.desc: King Arthur, accompanied by his squire, recruits his Knights of the Round Table, including Sir Bedevere the Wise, Sir Lancelot the Brave, Sir Robin the Not-Quite-So-Brave-As-Sir-Lancelot and Sir Galahad the Pure. On the way, Arthur battles the Black Knight who, despite having had all his limbs chopped off, insists he can still fight. They reach Camelot, but Arthur decides not to enter, as "it is a silly place".

programme.title: Idiocracy
programme.desc: Corporal Joe Bauers, a decidedly average American, is selected for a top-secret hibernation program but is forgotten and left to awaken to a future so incredibly moronic that he's easily the most intelligent person alive.

programme.title: Young Frankenstein
programme.desc: An American grandson of the infamous scientist, struggling to prove that his grandfather was not as insane as people believe, is invited to Transylvania, where he discovers the process that reanimates a dead body.

programme.title: Home Alone
programme.desc: Eight-year-old Kevin McCallister makes the most of the situation after his family unwittingly leaves him behind when they go on Christmas vacation. When thieves try to break into his home, he puts up a fight like no other.

programme.title: Ferris Bueller's Day Off
programme.desc: A brash, cocky high school senior, tired of skipping school to spend a boring day at home, is determined to enjoy an epic day roaring around his favorite Chicago sites, enlisting his best friend and girlfriend to join him on the adventure.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Frankenstein
programme.desc: The Wolf Man tries to warn a dimwitted porter that Dracula wants his brain for Frankenstein monster's body.

programme.title: Monty Python's Life of Brian
programme.desc: Brian Cohen is an average young Jewish man, but through a series of ridiculous events, he gains a reputation as the Messiah. When he's not dodging his followers or being scolded by his shrill mother, the hapless Brian has to contend with the pompous Pontius Pilate and acronym-obsessed members of a separatist movement. Rife with Monty Python's signature absurdity, the tale finds Brian's life paralleling Biblical lore, albeit with many more laughs.

programme.title: Spaceballs
programme.desc: When the nefarious Dark Helmet hatches a plan to snatch Princess Vespa and steal her planet's air, space-bum-for-hire Lone Starr and his clueless sidekick fly to the rescue. Along the way, they meet Yogurt, who puts Lone Starr wise to the power of "The Schwartz." Can he master it in time to save the day?

programme.title: Halloween III: Season of the Witch
programme.desc: After a terrified toy salesman is mysteriously attacked and brought to the hospital, clutching the year's most popular Halloween mask, Dr. Daniel Challis sets out to investigate the strange events and finds himself thrust into a nightmarish conspiracy.

programme.title: Bride of Frankenstein
programme.desc: Baron Henry Frankenstein and his monster both turn out to be alive, not killed as previously believed. Frankenstein wants to get out of the evil-experiment business, but when mad scientist Dr. Pretorius kidnaps his wife, Frankenstein agrees to help him create a new creature, a woman, to be the companion of the monster.

programme.title: Creature from the Black Lagoon
programme.desc: A scientific expedition searching for fossils along the Amazon River discovers a prehistoric Gill-Man in the legendary Black Lagoon. The explorers capture the mysterious creature, but it breaks free. The Gill-Man returns to kidnap the lovely Kay, fiancée of one in the expedition, with whom he has fallen in love.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Five grisly tales from a kid's comic book about a murdered father rising from his grave, a bizarre meteor, a vengeful husband, a mysterious crate's occupant, and a plague of cockroaches.

programme.title: Dracula
programme.desc: Transylvanian vampire Count Dracula bends a naive real estate agent to his will, then takes up residence at a London estate where he sleeps in his coffin by day and searches for potential victims by night.

programme.title: Ed Wood
programme.desc: Ambitious but troubled movie director Edward D. Wood Jr. tries his best to fulfill his dreams despite his lack of talent.

programme.title: Frankenstein
programme.desc: The 1931 horror movie "Frankenstein", tells the tale of when Henry Frankenstein who is a doctor, trying to discover a way to make a living human using human body parts from the recently deceased. He succeeds, but his creation is looked upon as a monster by the local inhabitants, who wish to destroy it.

programme.title: Frankenstein Meets the Spacemonster
programme.desc: When an atomic war on Mars destroys the planet's women, it's up to Martian Princess Marcuzan and her right-hand man Dr. Nadir to travel to earth and kidnap women for new breeding stock. Landing in Puerto Rico, they shoot down a NASA space capsule manned by an android. With his electronic brain damaged, the android terrorizes the island while the Martians raid beaches and pool parties.

programme.title: Frankenstein Meets the Wolf Man
programme.desc: The resurrected Wolf Man, seeking a cure for his malady, enlists the aid of a mad scientist, who claims he will not only rid the Wolf Man of his nocturnal metamorphosis, but also revive the frozen body of Frankenstein's inhuman creation.

programme.title: Hold That Ghost
programme.desc: Two bumbling service station attendants are left as the sole beneficiaries in a gangster's will. Their trip to claim their fortune is sidetracked when they are stranded in a haunted house along with several other strangers.

programme.title: House of Dracula
programme.desc: A scientist working on cures for rare afflictions, such as a bone softening agent made from molds to allow him to correct the spinal deformity of his nurse, finds the physical causes of lycanthropy in wolf-man Larry Talbot and of vampirism in Count Dracula, but himself becomes afflicted with homicidal madness while exchanging blood with Dracula.

programme.title: House of Frankenstein
programme.desc: After escaping from prison, the mad Dr. Gustav Niemann and his loyal hunchbacked assistant, Daniel, revive not only Count Dracula, but the Wolf Man and Frankenstein's Monster as well, all in order to exact revenge upon Niemann's many enemies.

programme.title: The Old Dark House
programme.desc: An American who sells cars in England receives a mysterious invitation from an old, eccentric millionaire to visit his house in which he lives with his twin brother.

programme.title: House of Wax
programme.desc: A sculptor opens a wax museum to showcase the likenesses of famous historical figures, but quickly runs into trouble when his business partner demands the exhibits become more extreme in order to increase profits.

programme.title: I Bury the Living
programme.desc: Through a series of macabre "coincidences," the newly-elected director of a cemetery (Richard Boone) begins to believe that he can cause the deaths of living owners of burial plots by merely changing the push-pin color from white (living) to black (dead) on a large wall map of the cemetery that notes those plots.

programme.title: Invaders from Mars
programme.desc: A young boy learns that space aliens are taking over the minds of earthlings.

programme.title: Invisible Agent
programme.desc: The Invisible Man's grandson uses his secret formula to spy on Nazi Germany.

programme.title: Island of Terror
programme.desc: A small, isolated island community's terrorised from a horde of creeping, blobbish, tentacled monsters which liquefy and digest the bones from living creatures. The community struggles to fight back, against these horrors.

programme.title: ACDC - Live at Caste Donington, England (August 17, 1991)
The Best of Ringo Starr and his All Star Band so far
Buck Cherry and Some Cheryl Crow Woodstock 99
Tom Petty and The Heartbreakers - Storytellers (1999)
Led Zeppelin Live Madison Square Garden
The Rolling Stones Live From Rio
The Eagles - Don Kirshner's Rock Concert (1974)
Columbo
programme.desc: Brilliant photographer Paul Galesko plans to murder his domineering wife, Frances, and pin the crime on an ex-convict named Alvin Deschler. His plan is to make it look as if Alvin kidnapped his wife and killed her before picking up the ransom money.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Walter is late for a special dinner with Maude because he is trying to stop his book keeper from committing suicide. However, while he is there, Walter has a heart attack and he ends up having much more than his health to worry about, when Maude discovers where he was.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: With Florida gone, Maude has finally decided to hire a new housekeeper. She is excited with the fact that the new one, Mrs. Naugatuck is British but quickly the two of them clash over gender roles, as Mrs. Naugatuck insists on calling Walter the master of the house and begins to pamper his every need.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: The girls try to cough up enough money to pay for a new roof. Meanwhile, Sophia's volunteer job at the hospital is what brings the girls a piece of inside information that could give them enough money for a new roof and money left to spare. The info is that a famous artist is dying, so the girls head to an auction to bid on one of his paintings.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: After being dumped by her boyfriend, Blanche befriends a man at a bar and makes a date with him unknown to her he's blind. Meanwhile, Dorothy and Rose try their hand at coaching a kids' football team.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Rose is crazy about her latest boyfriend, Ernie, but can't understand why he doesn't approach the subject of sex, so when she forces the subject she learns the truth - he's impotent.

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.desc: A lawyer comes to the hotel saying he represents, Sophia'a former son-in-law, Stan and he tells them that he died and that Stan's final wish is for them to be at his service. Later when Sophia's alone, Stan shows up and tells her that he's about to face criminal charges so to avoid them, he faked his death and will be going away.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred gets news that an uncle has died and has left him $1500 in his will. The only thing is, in order to get the money, Fred has to fly to St. Louis to claim it. However, first he must overcome a fear of flying and Lamont is up in arms trying to help him.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.sub-title: The Members of the Wedding (a.k.a.) The Engagement
programme.desc: Fred and Donna enjoy an evening out on the town but then Donna breaks the news that she has had another man propose marriage to her. Not wanting to lose her, Fred decides to propose marriage himself and they plan a small wedding ceremony. However, Lamont is totally against the whole thing and has Aunt Esther and family confront Donna and question her intentions.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Archie is caught between sympathy and self-preservation when the "other woman" from his past really needs his help. Her name is Denise and she's the attractive waitress who was the other half of an evening that Archie - and Edith - will always remember.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Edith is arrested for passing phony $10 bills she got from Archie's Place.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: After Mike and Gloria cancel their trip home for Christmas, the Bunkers decide to travel west for the holidays.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Kathy & Kari vs. Debi & Eileen vs. Julie & Adam
programme.desc: Gourmet Week, Day 1

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Jill & Jerry vs. Rosie & Maria vs. Trina & Keith
programme.desc: Gourmet Week, Day 2

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are fix-it men who are brought before a judge on a charge of chicken stealing. They escape from the courtroom and wind up getting hired in the judges' house after their antics attempting to fix the doorbell cause the servants to quit. The boys are discovered when the cake they bakes explodes all over a political supporter of the judge and he loses his chance for re-election

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are frontier guides leading a minstrel show west. When hostile Indians run the horses run off they are stranded. They must contend with a snow storm and a marauding bear as well the Indians. After almost killing each other ice fishing they solve their problems by rigging up a sail on the wagon and sailing west. A wagon train full of show biz girls tours the West, and the Stooges are assigned to protect them from Indians. First appearance by Stooge player Dorothy Appleby (former Miss Maine).

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are tree surgeons who are enlisted by a rich old man to find a mate for his rare puckerless persimmon tree. The boys sail to the tropical island of Rhum-Boogie to find the tree. When they arrive they are captured by the natives and will be eaten unless Curly marries the Chief's ugly daughter. The stooges escape with the tree and, after a confrontation with an alligator, sail off with their prize.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are living with their mother who persuades them its time to leave home and seek their fortune. After a con man sells them a phony deed to a lost gold mine, the boys head west to find the treasure. After some mishaps with Curly's gold finding invention, they locate the mine and strike it rich. When two crooked miners try to take their gold they hole up in an abandoned hotel and, although they get bombarded by dynamite, triumph over the crooks

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges run a drug store and are about to have their lease taken away by the Flint, the mean old man who owns the place. When Flint kicks his wife out for being old, the stooges try to help her by inventing a formula that makes old people young. Their concoction turns the wife into a beautiful young woman, and Flint offers the boys the store for free if they'll transform him as well. They agree, but after he swallows the stuff he turns into an infant, and the boys leave on the run.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Would be detectives, the stooges get a job guarding a Scotch castle while the owner is away. The servants are crooks intent on robbing the castle of its valuables. Though they do their best to frighten the boys off, the stooges prevail and expose the crooked goings-on.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are taking care of their invalid friend Mary who is confined to wheelchair. What they don't is that Mary is only faking her disability to swindle the insurance company. When the boys witness a hypnotist, "The Great Svengarlic", doing his act on the street, they think he might be able to hypnotize Mary so she can walk. Instead, they become subjects for his show and are hypnotized into walking out on a flagpole high above the ground. When they come out of their trance and realize their predicament, they fall into a window, startling Mary, who jumps from her wheelchair just as the insurance adjuster is about to hand her a check

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne's station wagon is hit by a luxury car that belongs to the rich Mrs. Wellman.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: On April 15th, Dan and Roseanne offer the kids a frantic lesson in family accounting; and in despair finally visit the local IRS office.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: In an attempt to bond with their daughters, Dan goes clothes shopping with Becky, and Roseanne watches basketball with Darlene.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne finally has a place to write when Dan builds her an office for her birthday -- but she's stricken with a case of writer's block.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Becky is eager to practice for her driving test, and the rest of the family awaits the results of Roseanne's pregnancy test.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Lending money to pal Arnie comes easily for Dan, but he believes that borrowing from family only adds up to disaster.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne decides it's time to give Becky "the talk", unaware that it's Darlene who needs it more.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Despite her misgivings, Roseanne, working late as a coffee-shop waitress, leaves Dan in charge of the kids.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Captain Peacock is celebrating his 20th year at Grace Brothers, and while his request for a raise has been turned down, he has been given a key to the Executive Wash Room and the privilige of eating in the Executive Dining Room. He lords this over the department staff and Mr. Grainger decides to make some trouble.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The department staff all fear catching a cold. Ms. Brahms swaps counters with Mr. Lucas to avoid catching anything from Mrs. Slocombe; meanwhile Mr. Lucas hopes to fake an illness so he can go home early.

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.desc: Bill and Toony get a surprise visit from a very old friend. Featured cartoons : Gorilla My Dreams (1948), I Never Changes My Altitude (1937), Goggle Fishing Bear (1949), Sink Pink (1965), Chaser on the Rocks (1965)

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: An old lady who writes popular mystery novels strongly suspects that her niece's husband murdered her, so she exacts revenge by asphyxiating him in her house safe while devising a solid alibi. It's up to Columbo to unravel the subterfuge.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline's ex Andy returns and she is denial over how they broke up. And when he informs her that he's about to get married, she offers to make the wedding cake. And she is constantly wondering if she made a mistake breaking up with him. And she tries to find wrong with him but can't.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline's cupcake shop is boycotted after they refuse to sell cupcakes to "I", an artist who wants to put cocktail weenies in them.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Han insists the diner gang attends a mandatory night out in an "Escape Room Club" when he feels they aren't working together as a team. However, once they're locked inside the "Alice in Wonderland"-themed room, they find Han has an ulterior motive.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: The Rockers face The Hart Foundation in the main event. "The Widow Maker" Barry Windham, Dusty Rhodes, Brutus “The Barber” Beefcake, “Hacksaw” Jim Duggan and more in action. Koko B. Ware debuts a new song.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.sub-title: The Cook, the Steve, the Gayle, & Her Lover
programme.desc: When Bob throws a dinner party to bond with his new friend, Gayle uses the opportunity to introduce the family to her new love interest. Little does she know, Louise has a long-standing feud with Gayle’s boyfriend and her niece will stop at nothing to break them apart. Calamity ensues as Bob attempts to impress his new buddy and the kids take action.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Ray's upper class in-laws come for a visit, he begs his parents to be nice to them. Ray admits to his parents that he doesn't always see eye-to-eye with Debra's folks but manages to fake it, and he'd like his parents to do the same when they all go out and try to have a family dinner.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray gets tired of being treated like a kid at his father's poker game, so he decides to ignore his father's advice and ends up losing all his money.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie's work has an annual golf trip, but Doug and Carrie used to always take Deacon and Kelly. Now that Deacon and Kelly have split up, Doug and Carrie have to try to find another couple to invite to the golf trip.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug starts to dream more seriously about running his own sandwich shop now that his Uncle Stu has generously offered him a large sum of money to see him realize his dream. However, Carrie has major reservations about this, knowing that Doug's gifts are not in running a business. Not to mention that Danny, Stu's son, is very jealous and angry that his own father would back Doug's business venture, but not his own.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George goes to work for his father; Jerry's girlfriend wants to see his emotions; Kramer adopts a suburban feel at home; Elaine prompts two Jewish men to renounce their faith.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Kramer stores his own blood to avoid a blood-bank fee; George tries to combine his two passions, sex and food; Elaine's plan to show responsibility backfires; Jerry trains with fitness guru Izzy Mendelbaum.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.sub-title: Do You Know Where Your Parents Are?
programme.desc: Since the girls continually break curfew, Dan and Roseanne celebrate the anniversary of their first lovemaking by revisiting the restaurant/hotel and staying out very, very late, hoping to teach the girls a lesson, with the help of Officer Jackie.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: An angry and deflated Roseanne discovered that her mother always thought sister Jackie had more potential.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: The Dunder Mifflin Infinity website is launching and Michael is excited about going to the big launch party in New York while Angela plans a satellite party for the Scranton branch. Meanwhile, Dwight competes against the website to see who can sell the most paper in one day.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: As Jan renovates the condo, Michael confronts his growing debt every way he can, which includes getting a second job. Pam and Jim spend a night out on Dwight's family beet farm, now a bed and breakfast.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart is fearing that he will fail an intelligence test, Bart switches exams with Martin Prince. When school psychologist Dr. Pryor studies the results, he thinks that Bart is a genius, and Bart is enrolled into a new school for genius kids. Bart doesn't fit in very well though.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart's class is on a field trip in the nuclear power plant in Springfield, where his father Homer works. When he breaks a pipe in the building, he is fired from his job, making him miserable. Homer is suddenly desperate for money and steals Bart's piggy bank for money, but all there is are a few cents. Homer feels guilty and he has the thought to threaten to kill himself. But Homer's family stops him and they tell him how much they love him.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When the Simpsons go to Mr. Burns mansion for a party, Homer looks at all the families that are happy and spend time together. Homer becomes embarrassed that his family isn't as happy as other families, so he finds ways to make his family happier. He eventually finds a family therapist that makes families get along. But it's hard to get the Simpsons to make a family as one.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Terrorized by the school bully, Bart seeks counsel from Grandpa Simpson, who takes him to a warfare expert for basic training.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa is sent home from school with a note exempting her from games because she is sad, though she perks up when she meets fellow saxophonist Bleeding Gums Murphy and Marge encourages her to express her moods. Homer, meanwhile, sick of always beaten by his son at video games, finds a boy who can teach him to win but, just as he is about to triumph, Marge unplugs the television leaving Bart's victories unchallenged.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Having filled in an application for the Rhoades' to be on a game show, Al and Peg appear on the game show for newlyweds - "How Do I Love Thee", portraying Steve and Marcy.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: After going with Steve to the racetrack, Al is reluctant to reveal that he won $1,200, because he knows that Peggy and the kids will take the money for themselves. While Al tries to throw off Peggy's suspicions with sex, Steve asks for increasingly exuberant bribes of Al's racetrack winnings so he will keep his mouth shut about the money.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Peg's hillbilly relatives from W* County pay a visit. Fighting uncles, singing triplet aunts, whose showbiz career is threatened by Al's inputs, and Al is trying all he can to watch the John Wayne film "Hondo."

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: In the third season opener, Al learns that he has had a copy of "The Little Engine That Could" since 1957 and when he goes to return it, he finds the same fat librarian that was there in 1957.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: A suspicious Al and Marcy question Peggy's claims that she saw Elvis Presley outside the shoe store at the mall, until Marcy notices that one of Al's shirts has a sweat stain in the shape of the King's face. When Marcy brings the news to her Elvis fan club, the Bundy house is soon overrun by Elvis fans and impersonators, driving Al to stay at the shoe store.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: A young football player named Matt, from Polk High, where Kelly and Bud go to school, is threatening two Bundy records: one held by Al for the most touchdowns in one season and the other by Kelly, by refusing her advances. So, Kelly resorts to becoming a cheerleader for Polk High's team in order to catch Matt's attention during a big season-ending game.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Steve, Marcy and the Bundys head out to a cabin in the woods, but the merriment is halted by Kelly's PMS. The men leave for a bit to go out fishing and return to all three women menstruating and hostile. When a bear attacks the car, Al hilariously attempts to stop him.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont's Uncle George dies and in his will he gives Lamont $7,000 if he weds and has a child and names it after his uncle and he has a year to do all this. Lamont has decided not to get married any time soon but blinded by dollar signs, Fred sneaks down to a dating service to match Lamont up with an available woman. Lamont catches on to Fred's plan and makes up a plan of his own to intervene.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: It's the 35th anniversary of Sanford & Son Salvage and Lamont has two gifts planned to give Fred. The first is a new window with the specially printed title of their business and the other is an expensive ring that everyone has chipped in to buy. However, when Fred runs across it, he becomes convinced that it was stolen from Frank Sinatra, after Frank's hotel room was ransacked by the Tip-Toe Bandit.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Grady is bursting with news and breaks it to Fred and Lamont that he's engaged to a woman he met at J.C. Penny. However, Fred and Lamont are shocked to discover the "raging beauty" Grady raved about is not exactly a beauty queen. Fred soon finds Grady spending all his time with her and sets a plan into motion to break them up.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred's friends Julio and Ah Chew are getting on Fred's nerves and Lamont takes on the challenge of trying to change Fred's bigotry towards minorities. When Lamont fails, he decides to move out and in with Julio, something that unnerves Fred who doesn't seem to get use to the arrangement.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris tries to raise money to help save a playground, so he tries to set a world record by having the most things piled on top of him.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: The newspaper fires all the paper boys and replaces them with the Paperboy 2000, a paper delivering robotic vehicle, so Chris challenges it in a paper delivering contest.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: While on vacation, Bugs and Daffy take a wrong turn and get lost in the Himalayas. There they encounter the Abominable Snow Man.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Sylvester Cat thinks he has finally ate and killed Tweety. Because of this he suffers terrible remorse.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Sylvester Cat decides to take his son, Junior, on a fishing trip to an aquarium.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Speedy's cousin, Slowpoke Rodriquez, comes to visit and puts Speedy in danger of being caught by Sylvester. Speedy beats of Sylvester by putting Tabasco sauce in his mouth and Slowpoke hypnotizes him into being their slave for the night and has him fix them a snack.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Wile E. Coyote tries and fails to catch the Road Runner using a bear trap with a bird seed bait, a jet rocket, an ice-making machine, and a boomerang.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Pepe Le Pew is in Paris, where the smell of his odor sends a female cat up a freshly painted flagpole, which puts a white stripe on her back and causes Pepe to think she is a girl skunk. He pursues her all around the Louvre.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Yosemite Sam marries a widow for her money, and once the honeymoon is over, the woman reveals her real bossy, loud mouthed ways.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Granny buys Tweety jet-propelled cage. Sylvester attempts to catch him.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: An alien, bored of his perfect home world, travels to Earth.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Middle-aged Beavis and Butt-Head get a warehouse job at the world's biggest internet retail company. You know the one we mean.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head go to a lingerie store and feel up the merchandise.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head try to harvest honey from a wasps' nest.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys want to sue a chick in class for sexual harassment because she's constantly giving them ""wood.""

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
The boys' class takes a field trip to an art museum.
videos:
Nirvana - "Heart Shaped Box"
Candyland - "Bitter Moon"
Matthew Sweet - "Superdeformed"

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Smart Beavis and Smart Butt-Head visit Earth and abduct Tom Anderson for human experiments; Beavis and Butt-Head are invited to sleep over at Cody's house when his parents mistakenly think they're good friends.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Butt-head rushes Beavis to the emergency room after an accident involving smoke, fire...and Beavis' butt.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head try to grow their own nachos as part of a school project.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Stewart has diarrhea; too much sugar turns Beavis into "The Great Cornholio."

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys get jobs as telemarketers; Beavis becomes the top salesman, and Butt-head can't figure out what to do.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butthead get called into work on a busy day. Butt-head gets out of it and sends Beavis in by himself, then goes in to harass him, and other customers as the restaurant comes to a standstill.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys photocopy a dollar bill and try to use the copies to buy stuff.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: The secret word of the day is "over." Cowntess shows home movies of her trip to Europe. A puppy shows up at the Playhouse, and Pee-wee names him Aloysius. Jambi is allergic to him. When Reba the mail lady shows up, they find out that Aloysius is really her lost puppy... They are reunited, and Pee-wee shows us how to make "macaroni art" with a portrait of the puppy.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Pee-wee and Cowboy Curtis take a leap into Magic Screen where they enjoy some mighty fine handcart ridin' until a train chases them back into the waiting arms of Chairry! Then, Cowboy Curtis sings a song with "more" of the secret word in it than you could scream at. Finally, Cowboy Curtis and Miss Yvonne go shopping at Pee-wee's "department store" where they decide to buy Chairry!

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Not Regular Down There
programme.desc: Max is both puzzled and curious when the new guy she is dating expresses hesitations about intimacy because he is "not regular down there." Also, Sophie and Oleg struggle to get pregnant.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: David Scatino, the father of Meadow's friend Eric and the owner of a local sports store, gets on the bad sides of both Richie and Tony when he loses big in two poker games. Paying his respects at a funeral, Tony is forced to deal with the presence of Livia.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: With Anthony Jr.'s confirmation approaching, Tony and Carmela find their son's growing apathy a cause for concern. Meanwhile, Christopher's interest in show biz is reignited when he meets a well-known filmmaker through his cousin's girlfriend.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Michael's grand scheme hits an unexpected snag and forces him to take drastic measures.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: A surprising announcement from one of the humans threatens to dissolve the group.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: The group explores the three main branches of ethical thought.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: As ancient grudges resurface, Rhaenys suggests that Alicent may see reason while the men around her seek bloodshed. Ser Criston proposes a bold scheme. Daemon arrives at Harrenhal to raise an army for the Blacks.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Stannis arrives at Winterfell. Tyrion runs Meereen as Daario and Jorah go after Daenerys. Jaime and Myrcella leave Dorne. Jon sends Sam and Gilly to Oldtown. Arya challenges the Many-Faced God. Cersei confesses her sins.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: The fate of Jon Snow is revealed. Daenerys is brought before Khal Moro. Tyrion gets used to living in Meereen. Ramsay sends his dogs after Theon and Sansa. Ellaria and the Sand Snakes make their move. Cersei mourns for Myrcella.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: House and the team take on the case of Nick, a book editor who loses his inhibitions. The team realizes Nick has frontal lobe disinhibition, which causes him to speak his mind having no control over what he says and making him just like House. Meanwhile, House suspects Wilson and Taub are keeping something from him.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: Morgan works in a nursing home with a pet cat who only sleeps next to people if they are about to die - and does so with alarmingly accuracy. When it seems the cat has predicted her own death, Morgan, convinced she is about to die, fakes a seizure in order to get to House to have him diagnose her before it’s too late.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: At a lakeside retreat for married couples, Marge is determined to fix her marriage with Homer, but Homer is more determined to catch General Sherman, the freakishly large catfish that lives in the lake.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart is fearing that he will fail an intelligence test, Bart switches exams with Martin Prince. When school psychologist Dr. Pryor studies the results, he thinks that Bart is a genius, and Bart is enrolled into a new school for genius kids. Bart doesn't fit in very well though.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Burns' birthday is approaching and he longs for Bobo,the teddy bear he had as a child. Bobo has had an illustrious history,including flying the Atlantic with Lindbergh and being buried in Arctic ice,some of which is sold as ice cubes to the Simpsons so that Bobo is now Maggie's toy. Burns attempts theft,bribery and robbery to get his Bobo back but the family stand firm against him until finally Maggie,feeling sorry for him,offers him Bobo.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: In an idyllic planned community, Homer works for a new boss who has trouble with the government. Meanwhile, the rest of the family has difficulty dealing with the bucolic burg.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge arranges for her mother Jacqueline Bouvier to start dating Grandpa Simpson and all is going well until Burns cuts in and takes her away from Grandpa Abe. When he hears that she is getting married to Burns he goes to the church and,in a nod to the climax of the film 'The Graduate',runs away with her and,after she has declared that she doesn't want to marry either of them,they get on a bus together.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Santa's Little Helper and his mate She's the Fastest have twenty-five puppies but they are unhappy to be separated for sale so Burns bids for all of them and when the family refuses, sensing evil intentions, he steals them to kill and make a coat for himself. He even sings a song 'See My Vest' to celebrate the fact. However Bart and Lisa rescue the puppies and eventually Burns buys them to breed for racing.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The Lastest Gun in the West
programme.desc: While running away from a vicious dog, Bart meets Buck McCoy, a former Western film star. Bart begins hanging out with him and starts to idolize him. Bart wants to help McCoy stage a comeback, so he convinces all of the kids in town to become interested in the Wild West. McCoy appears on the Krusty the Clown Show, but the comeback flops when Buck begins drinking again and injures Krusty the Clown.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart resents Milhouse's new girlfriend; Homer gets the wrong subliminal tape, which has him gaining vocabulary, not losing weight.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A new mogul wins the hearts of the townspeople with a shower of cash. Jealous, Mr. Burns asks Homer to help make him a beloved billionaire too, and they hatch a plan to bring the Loch Ness Monster to Springfield.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: In "I Know What You Diddily-Iddly-Did" the Simpsons fear they have killed Ned Flanders. "Desperately Xeeking Xena" satirizes superheroes of yore with "Stretch Dude" Bart and "Clobber Girl" Lisa. And "Life's A Glitch, Then You Die" depicts the upcoming horror of Y2K.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: The Corporation forces "Stone Cold" Steve Austin to compete in a Gauntlet Match. The Rock looks to warm up for his WWE Championship opportunity as he goes one-on-one with Steve Blackman. Plus, D-Lo Brown introduces Ivory to the WWE Universe, DX reveals some special plans for St. Valentine's Day Massacre, and much more.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira is worried that racy photos from her past will jeopardize the campaign; Johnny asks for financial help; Alexis gets a job.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Moira meet with a key group of people, Alexis struggles with her new job, David offer's emotional support to his boss' daughter.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline are unable to pay the rent on their shop. A person offers to take their lease but Caroline thinks they should try to find the money. So she decides to ask her Aunt who's a successful businesswoman. But she refuses to help them. They try to ask her again but she's on medication and she reveals that she hates Caroline which is why she doesn't want to help her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline take office temp jobs. While Caroline tries to climb the corporate ladder, Max deals with a boss who makes inappropriate advances.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Han fires Caroline when she insults his diner, so Max tries to mend their relationship.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline is in a tizzy when she develops a rash after a romantic evening with her former boyfriend.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline are invited to be extras on a popular TV show shooting a scene in Han’s diner, but when Caroline catches the director’s eye, he offers her an actual role – with strings attached.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline’s father asks her to go on a talk show to defend him when a former employee threatens to defame him in a tell-all book.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: As Caroline and Max deep-clean part of the diner for Han before a health inspection, they make a discovery that could help relaunch their cupcake business; Sophie and Oleg break up after he cheats on her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Word about a shocking incident involving a celebrity brings customers to Max and Caroline's new cupcake window.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline tries to raise money for an expensive pair of new pants through an online fundraising site. Meanwhile, Max’s new smartphone comes between her and Caroline.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Kitty Kitty Spank Spank
programme.desc: When Max becomes smitten with an old stray cat that's been hanging around the apartment, Caroline tries to convince her they need to find it a better home. But just when they think they've found one, the cat's fate takes a turn.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max and Caroline can’t figure out how to use the new cappuccino maker they bought for their cupcake business, they take temporary side jobs as baristas at a coffee house to learn the ropes.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max and Caroline start losing business to the new cronut craze (half-croissant, half-donut), they come up with their own trend – cake fries.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Feeling guilty for buying hair extensions with their joint funds, Caroline splurges on a new bedding set for Max but gives the old one to charity, not realizing her old pillowcase has sentimental value.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: After Han emails photos of a stripper’s face to his mother claiming she’s his girlfriend, he begs Max and Caroline to find her and convince her to play his girlfriend when his mother arrives from Korea.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Caroline’s date is a no-show after promising to take her to one of the city’s hippest new restaurants, she and Max decide to take advantage of the hard-to-get reservation.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: The girls get a cleaning woman to clean the apartment and the woman unearths an application Max has for pastry school. Caroline urges to go and apply but Max doesn't think she can get in. They go and Caroline finds herself attracted to the instructor Nicolas. He asks Max to make a tart as her project to see if she can get in. Max thinks it's a piece of cake till she finds out what a tart is.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: After a tragic football accident devastates a normally bubbly Dani Rojas, AFC Richmond brings in a sports psychologist. Meanwhile the team battles a seemingly unending tie-streak and Keeley tries to point a newly retired Roy in a new direction.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: Ted is surprised by the reappearance of a familiar face. Roy tries out a new gig.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: Rebecca has a special visitor shadow her at work. A player's return is not welcomed by the team.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman untangles the mystery behind cats and their nine lives, and demonstrates the dynamics of dynamite.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman explores the functions of the human mouth, and what causes bad breath. Phoebe has fun with worms. Gulliver solves the mysteries of the visual scale.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman explains what constitutes a catalyst and how an aerosol can operates.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman reveals the characteristics of rubber, how flowers bloom and uncovers the mysteries of hair growth.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman reviews Bactrian and dromedary camels, covers the densities of gases and explores the Dead Sea.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: The color of your eyes, the shape of your nose, and the straightness (or curliness) of your hair depend on your genes. Not jeans the pants, but genes, the long strands of chemicals in your cells. Genes are like a blueprint for your body, and your cells follow the blueprint to build you. All living things have genes in their cells. You get your genes from your parents – half from your mom and half from your dad. Your parents got their genes from their parents, your grandparents. Living things pass down their genes from generation to generation. Genes from a mother’s egg cell mix with genes from a father’s sperm cell to make a complete set of plans for a baby. Baby humans, baby dogs, and baby plants all grow up to look like their parents because of genes.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Towers, teepees, castles, and condominiums – some kind of planning goes into all buildings, no matter how big or small. Architects are people who design buildings, and the areas around buildings. Usually architects draw on a computer or a big desk (a drawing board). The plans show the dimensions of all parts of a building. Architects try to design with the purpose of the building in mind, but even buildings used for the same thing can look very different.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball's good day turns bad to worse throughout his school day, much to the point where he believes he is "cursed".

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball creates a creature, made of every gross thing he could find, with a cannibalistic appetite.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Seeing Gumball in need of attention, Nicole hangs out with him at school, which becomes increasingly embarrassing for her son.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball wears a lucky helmet, but it tears his family apart.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: After Gumball's bullying problem, Tina Rex demands to have a fight with him.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The Watterson family fights over the remote when they realize everything they want to watch comes on at the same time.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin upset a giant.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Tobias compete for Penny's affection by putting on the lamest duel in history.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Mom tries to inspire Gumball to develop ambition.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball is jealous when he sees Leslie with Penny.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Cyborg tells the other Titans that TV wrestling is fake and tries to teach them about real wrestling.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans are transported inside a fantasy adventure game and must find the key to return home.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Raven teaches the other Titans how to dance properly but then they are captured by the Dance Demon.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans reminisce about the time they threw a surprise party for Cinderblock.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans try to remember how to be superheroes in order to stop The Brain.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: When Santa tries to take over Halloween, it's up to the Titans and a few ghouls to stop him.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Cyborg and Starfire booby-trap the Tower for safety.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Starfire wins a goldfish at the carnival and vows to keep it alive.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Batman and Commissioner Gordon check in on various happenings around Jump City.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: When Santa decides to quit, the Titans pitch in to save Christmas.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: On Beast Boy's Birthday, Starfire complains because she has never celebrated a birthday on Earth. Beast Boy allows her to celebrate her birthday on the same day as his, but Beast Boy and Starlite get mad at each other after facing the struggle of sharing a birthday.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Stimpy falls in love with his wonderful belly button and all the fantastic noises it makes. A voice calls him from his belly button and tells him that he can crawl in. Here he meets Jerry the Belly Button Elf, who puts him to work as a domestic servant.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: TV star Haggis MacHaggis (voiced by Alan Young) is kicked out of his mansion and thrown out onto the streets after Ren and Stimpy's cartoon show becomes more popular than his. Just as he plans to end it all, Haggis enlists in the help of two thugs-for-hire who help him sabotage Ren and Stimpy's show.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Desperate to join the Barrette Beret Girls, Ren and Stimpy have to prove themselves to the burly babes.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: In this wildlife documentary, Ren and his stupid assistant observe the natural behavior of the rare animal called the Lummox. Actually an obese, ignorant, dim-witted and unhygienic human, these massive land-beasts exhibit playfulness, curiosity, rage and most intriguing of all, courtship. Which of the two competing ignoramuses underpants has what it takes to win over the female of the species?

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc:
Annoyed by Stimpy's stupidity, Ren goes to live off as a hermit, and goes crazy after months of being alone. He is kicked out of the hermits union for having imaginary friends.
Segment: Untamed World: Field Guide - Stimpy, reprising his role as a nature show host from Untamed World, in the jungles of Miami, Florida.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: The chums seek refuge in a model home of the future which is awash with rare conveniences.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Stimpy has a friend in his face (like a tick), then Ren ends up with the friend's friend, which starts to annoy him to no end.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
After calling a phone sex number, Beavis & Butt-head decide to get their own 900 number.
videos:
Eve's Plumb - "Blue"
Barry White - "Put Me In Your Mix"
Low Pop Suicide - "Kiss Your Lips"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
During Buzzcut's swimming class, Beavis must save a drowning Butt-head, but is unable and Buzzcut intervenes. The boys then have to serve as CPR dummies.
Curve - "Missing Link"
Carnival Art - "Mr. Blue Veins"
R.E.M. - "Nightswimming"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
A blackout forces Beavis and Butt-head out of the house to find a place to watch TV.
videos:
Radiohead - "Creep"
Morrissey - "November Spawned a Monster"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
After watching "that Letter-dude" on TV, Beavis and Butt-head do a similar show for their class project.
videos:
Grim Reaper - "See You In Hell"
Donny Osmond - "Sacred Emotion"
Greta - "Fathom"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
After playing "Iron Justice," Beavis has a near death experience, and dreams of meeting St. Peter, and Butt-head trying to bury him. videos:
Gary Numan - "Cars" Frank Black - "Los Angeles"
Tom Waits - "I Don't Want To Grow Up"

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Rif Raffs (Sarah & Bret) vs. Gruesome Twosome (Tim & Cathy)

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Blue Devils (Kay & Jeff) vs. Masterminds (Basami & Jerome)

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Muppets Get Suffed will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine's dad makes her do work while hosting the show, so she keeps shovels a wheelbarrow full of dirt onto the set.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Dukes of Hazard Line Up for Gas will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross spends most of the show making Christine's life miserable by making her do many strange things like jump from an airplane without a parachute, forcing her to be shot, makes her sick by distorting the broadcast, has her stuffed into a trash can, and even has her mummified.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Captain Kangaroo Robs a Bank will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine goes on strike demanding a raise. She eventually gets it, but discovers that she won't get paid for the time she was on strike, even though she was still on the show.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Jaws meets the Love Boat will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine spends the entire show waiting for her boyfriend to call her, which he doesn't.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Six Million Dollar Man Rusts to Bits will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross tries to make money by collecting his family allowances. He also tries to adopt Christine, who is using an exercise bike and some special effects to travel around the world.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Hit and Run on Sesame Street will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Despite her constant attempts to explain safety rules, bad things keep happening to Christine including electric shock and decapitation.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Republicans vs. Democrats vs. Third Party
programme.desc:
MXC presents their first ever three-way... battle in honor of the Presidential electoral college entitled "Squeeze Out The Vote 2004" where it's the Republicans against the Democrats against the Third Party. Forget the backstabbing and name-calling because this time, the mud-slinging's for real!
Games include: Rock the Moat; Dark Horse Race; Pork the Barrel; The Rotating Surfboard of Political Suicide; Mudslingers; Log Jam

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The people who can't hold a job are facing off against the tree-huggers who want to save the earth when the unemployed challenge the environmentalists in this MXC battle.
Games include: Great Holes of Glory; Finger It; Dope on a Rope; Irritable Bowl Syndrome; Pole Riders

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Snack Food Industry vs. Print Media
programme.desc:
If you're hungry for action, this episode of MXC is just for you. Members of the snack food industry challenge their arch rivals from the print media in a winner-takes-all battle for bragging rights.. .and a cold soda or two. Also, the Captain introduces everyone to his new book, "Well, You're Wrong!".
Games include: Slippery Slope of Slanted Death; Door Jam; Circle Jerkers; Brass Balls

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Baby product makers challenge the people who track down UFOs and ghosts on this edition of MXC in a war to settle their score, and nap time afterward for everyone.
Games include: Mud Butlers; Yankin' It; Flailing Wall; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Oral drillers take on spelunkers on this MXC affair when the dentists take on explorers. Also, Kenny demonstrates some of his new inventions... in which Vic needs some teamsters to help.
Games include: The Dash to Death; Hand Job; Wall Bangers; Sinkers & Floaters

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
MXC throws open their games to kids for our first "Children's Special" as the country children take on the city children and see where the best kids come from. Also, Vic and Kenny test out Vic's new invention... a career-choosing device for toddlers.
Games include: Escape From Foster Care; Lil' Sinkers and Floaters; Irritable Bowl Training; Rotating Surfboard of Death Jr.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Organized Crime vs. Weight Loss Industry
programme.desc: It's the classic battle of the feared mob vs. fat slobs as the Organized Crime takes on the Weight Loss Industry. Games - Dead Men Walking, Runaway Stump, Little Man in the Boat, and Tumbling Dominoes of Doom.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Novelty & Gift Industry vs. The Death Industry
programme.desc:
It's the people who make gag presents vs. the people that plant stiffs for profit in an aquatic-themed show. Meanwhile, Vic discovers the joys of Kenny Blankenship's dressing room.
Games include: Wet Spot; Circle Jerkers Ahoy; Elimination Idol; Wall Bangers

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Whenever Jane isn't painting or creating other works of art, she like to run. So in order to spite the wrath of a tyrannical P.E. teacher, meet a cute boy, and keep herself and Daria from getting a failing grade in gym, she goes out for Lawndale High's Track & Field team and becomes an instant jock, much to the dismay of Daria, the quintessential anti-jock.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: When Daria agrees to help Trent find a birthday present for Jane, they somehow end up at a piercing parlor offering a two-for-one special. Hmm.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria is assigned to write a short story turning people she knows into fictional characters. Like they're not unbelievable enough already.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The threat of a Lawndale hurricane causes people to start singing and dancing!

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: An obstruction by her glasses during a driving lesson causes Daria to contemplate trading them in for contact lenses. The trouble is, she fears that by doing so she will be viewed as being just as vain and superficial as the rest of her vapid fellow classmates.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The new spirit of "volunteerism" is mandatory at Lawndale High. While reading to senior citizens, Daria finds that her unenthusiastic tone makes her just as unpopular at the nursing home as at school.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: A fantasy show wherein Daria and Jane have to convince Christmas, Halloween and Guy Fawkes Day to return to Holiday Island.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Quinn gets roped into organizing the school dance; she fobs the job off on Jane.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: A Demon from the Fourth Dimension appears on Danger Mouse's doorstep to challenge the one-eyed white wonder to a duel. Before they can duke it out on the Demon's home turf however, the world's greatest secret agent is sidetracked or rather suckered into his own TV spectacular: 'This Was Your Life'.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Proffesor Von Squakenkluck has invented a time machine and accidentally sends Penfold back 150 Million Years into the past in place of a dinosaur egg. Danger Mouse follows in the Mark 12, after changing places with a Diplodocus.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: DM and Penfold have just retrieved a stolen secret fuel sample from Field Marchall Rommole. On their way back, the Mark 5 gets hit by an electric storm, Penfold is struck dumb and the two of them are shot into a distant future where Britain cats are the dominant species. Worse still, a distant relative of Baron Greenback rules the cats under the name of 'Big Leo'.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: Four Heads Are Better than Two
programme.desc: Colonel K has had his people create robot duplicates of Dangermouse and Penfold, so he can team the actual DM with robot Penfold and vice versa, in order to be able to stop more crime at the same time. The real Dangermouse and the fake Penfold are send to meet master of disguise Agent 57 at the Pinball Palace, where arch villain Baron Greenback is hatching a scheme to hypnotize the world by way of pinball machines.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: While climbing the rock of Gibraltar, Dangermouse and Penfold spot Baron GB's Frog Head Flyer and follow it to New York. There, they enter the International Oddbods Inc, an agency which represents some of the world's greatest crack-pots.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Baron Greenback and Count Duckula have teamed up in a quest to find the Great Bone Idol, which has the power to control every dog in the world. Greenback plans to use it to take over the world and Duckula would get Australia. This is besides the fact that Duckula has to do all the work. DM and Penfold head for the Himalaya's to stop the villainous duck.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Radio Control Plane - Square On Balloon - Armadillo Patient - Folded Paper Flips - Vectrex Drawing - Oldest Living Thing - Touch - Secret Inks - Fireproof Money

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Plastic Hot Air Balloon - Glue Powered Bugs - Apple Flower - Binary Number System - Lung's Capacity - Unbreakable Container - Spoon Into Glass - Suspended Book
